studyName: Genetic Markers of Caries Risk in Diverse Underserved Children: CIDR

tables.description: This subject phenotypes data table reports each subject's sex, ethnicity, race, age at visits 1 through 6, Medicaid status at visits 1 through 6, dental insurance status at visits 1 through 6, household income over the past 12 months at visits 1 through 6, frequency brushing with toothpaste at visits 1 through 6, frequency brushing teeth with non-fluoride toothpaste at visits 1 through 6, fluoride supplement use at visits 1 through 6, topical fluoride (varnish) use at visits 1 through 6, decayed, missing, and filled surfaces (DMFS) score using international caries detection and assessment system (ICDAS) severity greater than or equal to 1 at visits 1 through 6, decayed, missing, and filled surfaces (DMFS) score using international caries detection and assessment system (ICDAS) severity greater than or equal to 2 at visits 1 through 6, decayed, missing, and filled surfaces (DMFS) score using international caries detection and assessment system (ICDAS) severity greater than or equal to 3 at visits 1 through 6, decayed, missing, and filled surfaces (DMFS) score using international caries detection and assessment system (ICDAS) severity greater than or equal to 4 at visits 1 through 6, decayed, missing, and filled surfaces (DMFS) score using international caries detection and assessment system (ICDAS) severity greater than or equal to 5 at visits 1 through 6, status of natural teeth remaining at visits 1 through 6, and status of cavities, fillings, and/or teeth pulled in the last two years at visits 1 through 6.

tables.description: This pedigree data table contains family relationships in the format of family IDs, subject IDs, father IDs, mother IDs, and sex of subjects.

variables.name: FAMILY_ID
variables.description: Family ID

variables.name: FATHER
variables.description: Subject ID for Father

variables.name: MOTHER
variables.description: Subject ID for Mother

variables.name: MZ_TWIN_ID
variables.description: Twin ID for monozygotic twins and multiples. An MZ_TWIN_ID is not provided for dizygotic twins or multiples.

variables.name: SEX
variables.description: Biological sex

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Subject ID

tables.description: This subject consent data table contains subject IDs, consent information, biological sex, and aliases for subjects overlapping other dbGaP studies.

variables.name: CONSENT
variables.description: Consent group as determined by DAC

variables.name: SEX
variables.description: Biological sex

variables.name: SOURCE_SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Subject ID used in the source repository

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Subject ID

variables.name: SUBJECT_SOURCE
variables.description: Source repository where subjects originate

tables.description: This sample attributes data table reports each sample's body site of origin, analyte type, tumor status, and histological type.

variables.name: ANALYTE_TYPE
variables.description: Analyte type

variables.name: BODY_SITE
variables.description: Body site where sample was collected

variables.name: IS_TUMOR
variables.description: Tumor status

variables.name: SAMPLE_ID
variables.description: Sample ID

tables.description: This subject sample mapping data table includes a mapping of study subject IDs to sample IDs. Samples are the final preps submitted for genotyping, sequencing, and/or expression data. For example, if one patient (subject ID) gave one sample, and that sample was processed differently to generate 2 sequencing runs, there would be two rows, both using the same subject ID, but having 2 unique sample IDs.

variables.name: SAMPLE_ID
variables.description: Sample ID

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Subject ID
